claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're inquiring about.

Let me start with a few basic questions:

1. **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children are you asking about?

Once you let me know, I'll ask follow-up questions about each child's circumstances to determine eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm responsible for one child - my son Alex. He's 6 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'm assessing Child Benefit eligibility for your son Alex, who is 6 years old.

Let me ask you a few more questions to determine your eligibility:

**Does Alex live with you?** (Or if he doesn't live with you full-time, can you tell me about his living arrangements?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me. He lives with his mother.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since Alex lives with his mother, I need to understand your situation a bit more.

**Are you contributing towards Alex's upkeep?** For example, are you paying at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit rate towards his care (which is currently £25.60 per week for the eldest/only child)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not contributing financially at the moment. I'm not able to pay towards his upkeep right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for being honest about your situation. I have enough information now to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the Child Benefit rules:

- Child Benefit is normally paid to the person the child lives with
- If the child doesn't live with you, you can still claim Child Benefit if you're contributing to the child's upkeep at least the amount of the Child Benefit (currently £25.60 per week for an eldest or only child)
- Since Alex lives with his mother and you're not currently contributing financially towards his upkeep, you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for him

The Child Benefit claim would typically be made by Alex's mother, as she is the person he lives with.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: If your circumstances change in the future and you begin contributing financially to Alex's care (at least £25.60 per week), you may then be able to claim Child Benefit for him. If you have any other questions, please feel free to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), so meets the age requirement. However, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because: (1) Alex does not live with the claimant - he lives with his mother, and (2) the claimant is not contributing financially towards Alex's upkeep. Under Child Benefit rules, if a child doesn't live with you, you can only claim if you contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit rate towards the child's upkeep. Since neither condition is met, the claimant is not eligible.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 6), who lives with his mother. The claimant is not making financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep. Child Benefit is normally paid to the person the child lives with, and alternative claims require financial contributions equivalent to the Child Benefit rate. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Under Child Benefit rules, if a child doesn't live with you, you can only claim if you contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit rate towards the child's upkeep. Since neither condition is met, the claimant is not eligible.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 6), who lives with his mother. The claimant is not making financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep. Child Benefit is normally paid to the person the child lives with, and alternative claims require financial contributions equivalent to the Child Benefit rate. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
